title: ciliaria austromontana
intro: Ciliaria austromontana, also known as the Australian cilia pea, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern regions of Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ciliaria austromontana are said to include the reduction of inflammation and the improvement of symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Ciliaria austromontana can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, which are commonly consumed for its medicinal and health benefits.
intro_study: According to the "Saudi pharmaceutical journal : SPJ : the official publication of the Saudi Pharmaceutical Society", Ciliaria austromontana, which is likely a misnomer for Cenchrus ciliaris, has been shown to exhibit potential anticancer properties, particularly in its root extracts, which have been found to have a significant inhibitory effect on the growth of various cancer cell lines, including HepG-2, CACO, and A-549.
